headline: MATRADE briefed +9.3% trade and a 30.4% West Asia drop in one room. Press filed two stories.
context: On 21 April 2026, MATRADE chairman Reezal Merican told the same media briefing that March 2026 trade hit RM272.95 billion (+9.3% YoY) and Q1 Malaysia-West Asia trade fell to RM21.41 billion (-30.4% YoY).

cards.big: MATRADE briefed two figures from one room on 21 April: March trade up 9.3% to RM272.95 billion, Q1 West Asia trade down 30.4% to RM21.41 billion.
cards.sub: Malay Mail, NST, The Edge and Bernama filed two separate stories. No outlet joined them into one framing question. West Asia is only 2.7% of total trade.

cards.big: DOSM put Q1 2026 total trade at RM789.85 billion. The RM21.41 billion West Asia drop is 2.7% of that: a 30% fall in a sliver the aggregate barely registers.
cards.sub: Q1 exports hit a near-record RM426.53 billion (+12.7% YoY) on E&E demand. The trade surplus extended into a 71st straight month.

cards.big: Inside that number: palm oil to Iran collapsed 86.1% to RM90 million. UAE bilateral trade hit US$3.3 billion in H1 2025, up 30.9% under a CEPA in force since 1 October 2025.
cards.sub: Palm products were 62.4% of exports to Iran. The UAE deal targets US$13.5 billion non-oil trade by 2032.

cards.big: Reezal added a caveat: direct West Asia exposure stayed limited, but indirect costs (freight, war-risk premiums, goods prices) could weigh on competitiveness.
cards.sub: MATRADE rolled out an RM5 billion MSME export guarantee and 47 overseas Frontline Intelligence Hubs. Both sat below the +9.3% headline.

cards.big: When 2.7% of trade can fall 30% without moving the headline, the question is not how bad the collapse looks. It is who counts as a lost market when the aggregate does not.
A city reports downtown traffic up 9% in March while one neighbourhood loses a third of its shops. Both figures are true. The city map decides which one is news.
cards.sub: MATRADE published both. Press filed two stories. The choice to brief them as parallel narratives is itself the framing.

cards.big: Both figures are true. The diversification worked. The exporters who lost the channel are not in the headline.
